title: A Meteor Lit Up the Sky Like It Was Daytime
script: A meteor streaked across the night and lit up the whole sky like it was suddenly daytime. Cameras caught a blinding green fireball tearing overhead, casting shadows on the ground for a few seconds before it burned out. Most meteors are no bigger than a pebble; they just hit the atmosphere at over thirty thousand miles an hour, and the air in front of them compresses so hard it glows white-hot. Some ended with a sonic boom that rattled windows for miles. Scientists say a fireball this bright happens somewhere on Earth almost every night. So what are the odds a piece actually lands near you?
